title: Form columns not in correct order when viewing in Main Table
excerpt: I created a Monday Workform that when answers are submitted then populates to the Main Table. There are a bunch of questions and conditions in the form. However, when I go to the Main Table to check my answers, none of t…

posts.author: cvdw (Cait VanDeWiele)
posts.cookedHtml: I created a Monday Workform that when answers are submitted then populates to the Main Table. There are a bunch of questions and conditions in the form. However, when I go to the Main Table to check my answers, none of them are in the correct order. Is this a bug or did I do something incorrectly? I’ve spent DAYS working on this. Any help would be greatly appreciated.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Cait!

When you say things are in the wrong order, are you meaning the columns (aka “questions”) are being rearranged in the board? Or the items are coming in out of order?

The columns and items should just be arranged in the order they were created by default - unless you specifically rearranged them.

I wonder if you have a column sort option on? Or perhaps a specific sorted board view?

Would you be able to attach a screenshot (with no sensitive information visible, of course) and explain how it differs from what you set up so we can take a closer look?

posts.author: hollyvera (Holly Duque)
posts.cookedHtml: did anyone ever get a reply on here? The same thing is happening to me. I have a form and ordered the questions in a certain order but on the Main Table the columns are arranged in a completely different order. It even has questions that I previously had and then deleted.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @hollyvera This is expected behavior. The order of questions in a Form does not control the column order on the Main Table. The board keeps its own column order based on how columns were created, moved, or deleted.

To fix it, open the board and manually reorder the columns. If you see old questions, those columns likely still exist and may just be hidden.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard
